title: Mass. family, Worcester woman identified as victims in deadly Virginia bus crash
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Five Massachusetts residents, including a Greenfield family and a Worcester woman, died in a Virginia bus crash on May 29.
The bus crash occurred on Interstate 81, involving the Doncev family's vehicle and resulting in their deaths and 34 injuries.
Bus driver Jing S. Dong faces involuntary manslaughter charges for failing to slow down, causing the fatal collision.

title: Connor Wong’s 3-RBI night helped power Red Sox win, and it couldn’t come at a better time for him individually
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Connor Wong drove in 3 RBIs during the Red Sox game against Cleveland, aiding in a team victory.
Wong hit a double that was initially ruled a home run, leading to his first Wally head celebration.
Despite losing primary catching duties, Wong's performance could strengthen his case as secondary catcher.

title: Sonny Gray continues to be great addition to Red Sox rotation, earns 6th win vs. Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray pitched six innings in the Red Sox's 9-1 win against the Cleveland Guardians, allowing one run and four hits.
Gray's season ERA is now 3.06, with a 2.00 ERA over five May starts, contributing to a 7-3 Red Sox record in his starts.
Gray, earning $31 million this season, has a $30 million mutual option for 2027, influencing potential trade deadline decisions.

title: Here’s the full lineup of athletes and celebrities involved in Drake Maye’s charity softball game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Drake Maye's charity softball game will be held at Polar Park, Worcester, with a lineup of celebrities and sports stars.
Patriots coaches Mike Vrabel and Josh McDaniels will lead Team Patriots and Team Sox, featuring athletes like Julian Edelman and Zdeno Chara.
Proceeds from the event will benefit the MayeDay foundation, supporting children and families facing hardship.

title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: New England Patriots expected to trade for Eagles' A.J. Brown, with a framework reportedly in place.
The trade could finalize post-June 1 to ease Eagles’ salary cap hit from $43.5 million to $16 million.
Negotiations reportedly stalled as Patriots hesitate to trade a first-round pick, as sought by Eagles.

title: Did you hear it? Here’s what we know about a big boom heard around Mass.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A loud boom heard around Greater Boston on May 30 was caused by a bolide meteor entering the atmosphere.
Meteorologists ruled out thunderstorms and earthquakes, identifying a distinctive flash on satellite as the meteor's source.
Massachusetts officials reported no emergencies despite multiple 911 calls across the region regarding the incident.

title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning, leading to Red Sox's 4-3 loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, pitching seven scoreless innings, striking out five, and allowing no walks.
Red Sox are facing a pitching dilemma with Bello, who has a 0.71 ERA as a reliever but struggled as a starter.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised the passionate Celtics fans and TD Garden atmosphere during a recent livestream.
Boston Celtics and Miami Heat are considered top contenders to land Antetokounmpo amidst ongoing trade rumors.
Speculation includes potential trade involving Jaylen Brown if Celtics pursue Antetokounmpo in a blockbuster deal.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police dispersed Harvard's Senior Sunrise event due to crowding on Weeks Footbridge early Friday.
The event, attended by Harvard's Class of 2026, involved attempts to swim in the Charles River.
Cambridge and Massachusetts State Police responded; the legality of swimming was a key concern.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Four Seasons' Season 2, starring Tina Fey and Will Forte, is now streaming on Netflix.
'The Truman Show,' featuring Jim Carrey, is available on Paramount+ and YouTube.
'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is streaming on Peacock, showcasing Uma Thurman's notable performance.
